muon_NEW_INVARIANT: HARD MEM GATE (user, 2026-06-29): fused peak memory MUST stay <= baseline-mixed peak. Encoded in bench.py (mem gate PASS/FAIL line + frontier 'ok/OVER' annotation). This RETROACTIVELY REJECTS the Round-3 64M default (1155>891 @48t, 3830>3222 @192t) -> reverted default to 4M (862/3180 MB, both under baseline; 1.09x/1.05x). The speed/mem frontier is now a CONSTRAINED objective: fastest cap with peak<=baseline.

muon_round4_dtod.objective: cut the Memcpy DtoD (~11% of CUDA) while keeping peak mem <= baseline-mixed
muon_round4_dtod.diagnosis: DtoD count (3760 ~= 188/step) tracks baddbmm count (4000 ~= 200/step) almost 1:1. torch.baddbmm(input,A,B) maps to a cuBLAS gemm C=alpha*A@B+beta*C that needs C pre-loaded with the bias -> PyTorch emits a DtoD MEMCPY of `input` per call. The axpy-fold (the kernel's original design) has a hidden bias-copy cost.
muon_round4_dtod.candidate: fused-bmm (ns_variant='bmm'): plain bmm (beta=0, no bias-copy) + IN-PLACE axpy on the cache-hot gemm output. B=bmm(A,A).mul_(c).add_(A,alpha=b); X=bmm(B,X).add_(X,alpha=a). Mathematically identical, and CLOSER to the full-fp32 reference (which already uses separate b*A+c*A@A) so parity should match/beat baddbmm. Lower transient too (no separate bias buffer).
muon_round4_dtod.predicted: Memcpy DtoD ~-> 0 in the fused-bmm profile; some elementwise add_ kernels appear instead; net win bounded by overlap (compute-bound step may hide part of the 11%). Realistic 3-8%. Parity fused-bmm vs fp32 <= fused-mixed's. Mem gate PASS (<= baseline).
muon_round4_dtod.keep_gate: fused-bmm: ms < fused-mixed beyond noise AND parity ~<= fused-mixed AND peak <= baseline. If win -> promote ns_variant='bmm' to default. Else revert.

muon_round3_cudagraph.objective: minimize fused Muon step ms on T4 (baseline-mixed=1.00x); parity fused-fp32 vs full-fp32 < 1e-4, fp16-NS stability PASS, peak mem within reason
muon_round3_cudagraph.eval_verdict: python bench.py --compile muon (48 tensors/75M) AND muon_big (192/302M) on Tesla T4; user runs + pastes
muon_round3_cudagraph.diagnosis: Step is LAUNCH-BOUND: profiler 'Command Buffer Full' = 60% (48t) / 81% (192t) of CUDA — GPU stalls waiting on CPU to submit ~1787/~7087 tiny kernels. GEMM (baddbmm+bmm)~62% = recipe floor (3 matmuls/iter x5, can't cut). copy_+Memcpy DtoD ~24% data movement. Fusion already halved launches; residual launch tax gates everything and GROWS with scale (why 192t is only 1.03x).
muon_round3_cudagraph.hypothesis: CUDA-graph capture of momentum->NS->scatter collapses ~1787/7087 launches to (few eager grad-gathers)+1 replay, killing Command Buffer Full. Highest ceiling on muon_big (the real-training regime). Recipe math untouched -> parity preserved. Predicted: parity == fused-mixed (~2.3e-5), launch count collapses, ms approaches GEMM-bound floor (potentially 1.3-2x; theoretical since GPU spends ~50% in command-buffer stalls).
muon_round3_cudagraph.candidate: fused-graph (FusedMuon use_graph=True): eager grad-gather into persistent fp16 staging buffers (robust to grad rebinding) + captured compute body; try/except falls back to permanent eager (=fused-mixed) on capture error.
muon_round3_cudagraph.diagnosis_CORRECTED: LAUNCH-BOUND was WRONG. CUDA-graph collapsed launches 1768->830 and 'Command Buffer Full' vanished from the profile, yet wall-clock was unchanged (tie 48t, -1% 192t) and total CUDA time held (1.486->1.508s). The step is GPU-COMPUTE-bound on the small fp16 GEMMs (turing_fp16 gemm ~839ms + Memcpy DtoD 166ms, IDENTICAL in both profiles). 'Command Buffer Full' was CPU submission OVERLAPPED behind a saturated GPU, not idle stall. Removing launch overhead the GPU never waited on buys nothing. Consistent with the standing lesson: compile/HBM already saturated -> only a STRUCTURAL edge wins.
muon_round3_cudagraph.win_KEPT: ns_batch_elems default 4M->64M. T4 frontier knee: 48t 1.09->1.16x (862->1155 MB), 192t 1.06->1.10x (3180->3830 MB); matches uncapped speed at LESS memory (192t 3830 vs 4521). Mechanism: at 4M the (9,1536,512) expert stacks row-chunk (row_cap=5 -> 5+4); at 64M all 9 experts batch into ONE bmm = bigger GEMM = better SM utilization. Past noise, consistent across both sizes and both runs.
muon_round3_cudagraph.decision: KEEP 4M->64M default. DISCARD fused-graph from the bench variants/profile; KEEP use_graph as documented opt-in (bit-correct, would pay off on a launch-bound host). fullgraph=True on the NS baseline kept (fairer 1.00x bar).
muon_round3_cudagraph.next_lever: Memcpy DtoD = 11% of CUDA (166ms, 3760 calls, present in BOTH profiles) is the only remaining non-GEMM cost. Likely a contiguity copy of a transposed operand inside newton_schulz (bmm/baddbmm with X.transpose). NEXT round: find + eliminate it (bounded ~11% ceiling). GEMM 62% is the recipe floor (tl.dot refuted).

round4_conv_router.objective: max conv-router fwd+bwd x-vs-COMPILED-eager on T4, grad_rel<1.5e-2 idx-exact count-ok nan-free, mem<=compiled
round4_conv_router.eval_verdict: python bench.py --compile router on Tesla T4 (B16 S1024 H512 E11 K4 k2); baseline=compiled eager=the dump
round4_conv_router.eval_local: python bench.py router on RTX 3050 (BiBo .venv) — CORRECTNESS + relative order ONLY, NOT T4-comparable

backends.ref: dump recipe hand-built uncompiled (cuDNN conv+torch glue, pure-autograd)
backends.readonce: tldot fwd with H-outer/K-inner loop reorder (iter-1 attack)
backends.cublas: kept in code, dropped from sweep (dominated)

round4_conv_router.iter1_T4: ref fwdbwd 0.84x (bwd 1.20x WIN), tldot 0.71x, readonce 0.33x fwd REFUTED+REMOVED. Profiler: our tl.dot conv ~2.5x slower than cuDNN; aten::topk ~295us FLAT tax on all paths incl compiled.
round4_conv_router.iter2_T4: cudnn fwd+bwd 0.97x (BEST backend; tldot 0.73, ref 0.85), grad_rel 2.2e-7 exact, mem parity. topk ELIMINATED from profile. TIES compiled (not a clear >1.0x win).
round4_conv_router.iter3: cudnn micro-opt F.pad->conv1d(padding=K-1)[...,:S] (kills 16.8MB pad copy); default backend set to cudnn.
round4_conv_router.ceiling: our Triton conv (818us) ~= compiled WHOLE forward (821us); cuDNN owns the conv (inductor punts to it); bwd = same convolution_backward on all paths (tie). topk was the only fuseable seam -> took it. >1.0x needs beating cuDNN = not available.
round4_conv_router.status: CLOSED+SHIPPED: cudnn 1.11-1.17x on T4, integrated into BiBo (router_type=conv). repo cleaned to cudnn-only; refuted approaches + Ampere/Hopper revisit in conv_router_findings.md
round4_conv_router.transpose_free_round: CLOSED on T4 (64KB SRAM caps it). Documented for better hardware.

objective: minimize our CE fwd+bwd latency vs the Liger fused-linear-CE baseline at matched memory, grad_rel < 1.5e-2
eval_opt_set: .autoresearch/ce_eval_local.py on RTX 3050, proxy N=4096 V=32000 H=512 (autonomous; correctness + relative latency only — does NOT predict T4 occupancy)
eval_held_out: python bench.py --compile ce_sweep liger_ce_sweep on Tesla T4 (N=16384 V=81000) — user runs

baseline.name: Liger LigerFusedLinearCrossEntropy (the SOTA external kernel for this niche)

best.name: _CEFusedFwdBwd (grad-in-forward, no recompute; per-row _fwd_reduce + 2D-grid _grad_logits_inplace)
best.status: CONVERGED — beats Liger@1024 by 19% at +68MB AND Liger@2048 (faster, 180MB less); grads tighter to fp32 than Liger. Ported to BiBo src/kernels/fused_ce.py.

diagnosis: Round-2 'recompute GEMM is the irreducible floor' was REFUTED by the Liger baseline. CE grad needs only logits+labels (loss scalar -> scalar upstream grad), so the full gradient is computable in the FORWARD chunk loop -> no stored (N,V), no recompute (4 GEMM -> 3). The recompute was a self-inflicted design choice from splitting fwd(lse)/bwd. Occupancy sub-trap: a one-program-per-row fused reduce+grad kernel won locally but lost on T4 (launch-bound, V streamed serially); the 2D-grid grad kernel saturates the T4 SMs and is the real fix.
tried: BMM/grouped-GEMM for chunks REJECTED (needs full (N,V) resident = no memory win)
removed: _CECublasChunked recompute (dominated by fused, no mem upside)
stop_reasoning: CONVERGED. Ours Pareto-beats the SOTA external baseline (Liger) on speed AND grad accuracy at matched memory; compiled std CE remains fastest only when logits fit (CE is the memory/OOM play). No remaining lever clears Liger without spending the memory back (BMM = compiled). Shipped + ported to BiBo + docs updated.
